title: Plan agent template assumes research handoff exists — breaks Tier 2
description: The plan agent template in step-agents.md says 'Read .work/{name}/research/handoff-prompt.md — this is your primary input.' work-feature.md (Tier 2) dispatches to this same template, but Tier 2 has no research step — the file won't exist. The plan agent would fail trying to read a nonexistent file.

title: {beads_issue_id} used in prompts but state schema field is issue_id
description: work-fix.md and work-feature.md use {beads_issue_id} in agent prompt Task Context blocks, but the state.json schema in state-conventions.md names this field 'issue_id'. The step-agents.md variable table lists {beads_epic_id} but not {beads_issue_id} or {issue_id}. Variable would resolve to undefined.

title: Teams protocol teammate prompt has 7 sections instead of 6
description: Spec 00 section 1 defines exactly 6 sections (Identity, Task Context, Rules, Instructions, Output Expectations, Completion) with 'No additional sections.' The teammate prompt in teams-protocol.md adds a 'File Ownership' section between Output Expectations and Completion, making 7 sections. The file ownership content should be folded into Instructions or Output Expectations.

title: {beads_epic_id} variable null in Tier 2 dispatch preamble
description: The plan dispatch block in work-feature.md fills {beads_epic_id} from state.json. Tier 2 tasks never have an epic — beads_epic_id is always null. The agent prompt preamble would show 'Epic: null'. The preamble should conditionally omit the Epic line for Tier 2 or substitute with issue_id.

title: Delegate command skill injection doesn't follow spec 00 matrix
description: delegate.md injects work-harness for 'implementation/research/test' routes but not 'decompose'. Also injects work-harness for 'test' which has no equivalent in the spec 00 skill matrix. Should follow: plan/review = code-quality only; research/implement/write/create/test = code-quality + work-harness.

title: References to architecture-decisions.md without 'if exists' conditional
description: work-deep.md references .claude/rules/architecture-decisions.md in 5 places (research Phase B checklist, plan Phase B checklist, re-invoke instructions) without 'if it exists' conditionals. This file does not exist in the repo — it's optionally created during harness-init. Other commands (ama.md, phase-review.md) correctly use 'if it exists'.

title: work-deep.md research step uses frontmatter-style skill syntax in prose
description: Research step prose says 'Teammates get skills: [work-harness, code-quality] per the Step Routing Table.' This reads like YAML frontmatter syntax, which spec 00 section 3 explicitly says not to use. The teams-protocol.md template correctly uses Read instructions. The prose should reference the teams protocol rather than using frontmatter-like syntax.

title: Decompose agent template missing bash code fencing
description: In step-agents.md, the decompose template's bd create command and YAML frontmatter for stream execution documents are not wrapped in code fences. The spec 02 canonical template had these properly fenced. Missing fences could cause the agent to misparse instructions.

title: Teammate completion signal doesn't match spec 00 section 6
description: Spec 00 section 6 defines a structured completion signal: 'Step: {step} / Status: complete / Artifacts: ... / Summary: ... / Deferred: ...'. The teammate prompt's Completion section only says 'Mark your task as complete in the shared task list.' While teammates aren't full step agents, spec 05 says 'Teammate prompt template follows spec 00 section 1 structure' which includes completion signal.

title: step-agents.md scope description incomplete in work-harness.md
description: work-harness.md describes step-agents as 'Complete prompt templates for plan, spec, and decompose step agents.' This is accurate for the file's contents but creates inconsistency — some step agent prompts (research teammates, implementation agents) are defined inline in command files, not in step-agents.md. The description should clarify this scope.

title: context-seeding.md references 'spec 00 S3' instead of 'spec 00 section 3'
description: context-seeding.md uses 'spec 00 S3' as a section reference format. All other files use 'spec 00 section 3' or full names. Minor inconsistency in cross-referencing style.

title: Delegate command has no retry/feedback loop
description: Dispatch blocks in work-deep.md implement 'present, handle feedback, max 2 re-spawns' per spec 00 section 5. The delegate command simply returns output with no re-spawn option. This is by design (lightweight command, spec 06 is silent on retry), but a note suggesting re-run with more specific description would be helpful.

title: Dual skill injection patterns in work-deep.md
description: work-deep.md has both Skill Injection Path B fragments (for research/implement/review) and dispatch blocks referencing step-agents.md templates (for plan/spec/decompose). Two sources of truth for skill injection. A comment noting which source applies where would prevent maintenance confusion.

title: Docs call /delegate a 'skill' when it is a command
description: docs/feature/agent-first-architecture.md and docs/harness-roadmap.md refer to /delegate as a 'Skill'. It lives in claude/commands/delegate.md, making it a command. Should say 'Command' for accuracy.

title: Plan agent template assumes research handoff exists — breaks Tier 2
resolution: Identity softened to 'Create an architecture document'; Tier 2 fallback instruction added; {issue_id} added to variable table

title: {beads_issue_id} used in prompts but state schema field is issue_id
resolution: Changed to {issue_id} in work-fix.md and work-feature.md; added {issue_id} to step-agents.md variable table

title: Teams protocol teammate prompt has 7 sections instead of 6
resolution: File Ownership merged into Instructions step 5; Output Expectations section added; template now has exactly 6 sections

title: {beads_epic_id} variable null in Tier 2 dispatch preamble
resolution: Added '(if null, omit the Epic line from the preamble)' conditional note

title: Delegate command skill injection doesn't follow spec 00 matrix
resolution: Condition expanded to write/create/implement/research/test; added note that plan/design/review get code-quality only

title: References to architecture-decisions.md without 'if exists' conditional
resolution: Added '(if it exists)' to all 5 references in work-deep.md

title: work-deep.md research step uses frontmatter-style skill syntax in prose
resolution: Reworded to reference skill injection via Read instructions in the Rules section

title: Decompose agent template missing bash code fencing
resolution: Added 4-space indent to bd create command and YAML frontmatter block

title: Teammate completion signal doesn't match spec 00 section 6
resolution: Expanded Completion with numbered steps and structured return format

title: step-agents.md scope description incomplete in work-harness.md
resolution: Description clarified: dispatched step agents only, other steps inline

title: context-seeding.md references 'spec 00 S3' instead of 'spec 00 section 3'
Delegate command has no retry/feedback loop
resolution: Acknowledged as by-design per spec 06; no code change needed

title: Dual skill injection patterns in work-deep.md
resolution: Added clarifying note distinguishing dispatched vs non-dispatched skill injection paths

title: Docs call /delegate a 'skill' when it is a command
resolution: Changed 'Skill'/'skill' to 'Command'/'command' in both docs files
